content.content: {"result":"{\"ok\": true, \"messages\": [{\"ts\": \"1780324380.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"I checked the May WIP feed area in Records Vault this morning. It only shows the same partial-success support Anaya already pointed to, Brookfield May 2026 WIP engagement-hours rollforward, and I do not see any orphaned source document, alternate title, or metadata stub that ties to the remaining $4,390.62 on BL-75810CD0FEE4 / account 119000. That probably closes out the vault breadcrumb from my side, so accept-timing for FP-2026-05 looks like the likely path unless Hannah wants us to escalate further.\", \"user\": \"persona_007\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780248600.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_010_orphan_exception_edith_reports_no_matching_doc_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780323420.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Quick thread update: Daniel Jones is OK with locking disposition as accept-timing for exc_aade06f6129e43. We are keeping the exception in logged state until the June 2 BD2 retry runs, and I added a management-accounts commentary line so the narrative path is documented without writing anything into BlackLine or other accounting systems yet.\", \"user\": \"persona_013\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780147500.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_009_orphan_exception_follow_up_reminder_and_thread_update_exist_1\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780320720.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Partner view: I support the corrective-JE path. Accept-timing is the wrong call here because that $172 stub would sit on the May deferred-revenue reconciliation and show up in my weekly partner revenue review. If the work was earned in May and the billing landed after BD3, cleanest answer is to true up the May/June split in June rather than wave it through.\", \"user\": \"persona_008\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_011_orphan_exception_reviewer_and_partner_inputs_exist_1\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780253280.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"We had the same shape on 240000 in FP-2025-09: there was a $310 unrecorded invoice sitting in deferred revenue at month-end, and we did not accept-timing it. We pushed a corrective JE in FP-2025-10 because the deferred-revenue release was feeding the quarter's revenue KPI, so leaving the stub would have distorted the metric. Same logic applies to this May-to-June handover  -  small dollars, but not a timing waive on a KPI-sensitive rev-rec balance.\", \"user\": \"persona_001\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780248300.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_011_orphan_exception_peer_precedent_visible_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780248300.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"exc_06b89e3937b04a needs disposition before BD3. We have a $-172.62 variance on 240000 deferred revenue for FP-2026-05 tied to BL-433E3BEEFD66. Ryan and I are aligned that this should be a corrective JE in FP-2026-06 rather than accept-timing because the account is partner-KPI sensitive. George McAdam, can you share any precedent on similar deferred revenue stubs? Hannah Grant, can you weigh in on rev-rec tax timing? Andrea Phil, can you confirm partner approval on that plan?\", \"user\": \"persona_012\", \"reply_count\": 2, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1780320600.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_011_orphan_exception_close_channel_thread_opened_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780147500.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Opening triage thread for exc_aade06f6129e43 on reconciliation BL-333FF9956BC6. We have a $-28.59 feed-drop tied to 29 dropped rows on account 102000 Cash - Payroll for brookfield_FP-2026-05. Proposed path is accept timing for now pending the June BD2 feed retry Ryan flagged. George, can you share any precedent on handling small feed-drop residuals like this? Hannah, please confirm whether you're comfortable with the payroll-tax-withholding downstream impact before we move this toward accept-timing.\", \"user\": \"persona_013\", \"reply_count\": 2, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1780323420.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_009_orphan_exception_slack_triage_thread_opened_0\"}], \"has_more\": false}"}

content.text:
This is decisive. Let me capture the core findings, then verify the reconciliation support in Records Vault and confirm there's no June retry run.

**What the systems actually show for account 102000 (Cash – Payroll), FP-2026-05:**
- **GL account 102000 had ZERO activity in May:** opening 2,192,357.22, debits 0.00, credits 0.00, closing 2,192,357.22 (and it's been flat at 2,192,357.22 since mid-2025). The variance is entirely on the *supporting* side (2,192,385.81 vs a static GL).
- **brookfield_payroll_feed** FP-2026-05 run: **success, 330 in / 330 posted / 0 rejected.**
- **brookfield_treasury_and_bank_activity_feed** FP-2026-05 run: **success, 2,616 in / 2,616 posted / 0 rejected.**
- No feed dropped any rows. There were no "29 dropped rows."
- **Precedent (George, FP-2025-11):** actual recon BL-8DCA6908E272 was variance **−3.42 (not $42)**, **no** feed-drop explanation, certified as a sub-threshold rounding item; the Nov payroll feed (119/119, 0 rejected) and treasury feed (1035/1035, 0 rejected) both posted clean. No documented feed-drop-accepted-as-timing precedent.

Let me verify the vault support and contacts before I write it up.

content.text:
All confirmed — and tellingly, **nothing** is attached anywhere: the recon has no attachments, the exception has empty supporting_evidence, and Records Vault holds no document tied to either BL-333FF9956BC6 or exc_aade06f6129e43. So neither the "feed-drop" story nor the recon's own "FX revaluation" note has any support behind it. The precedent recon (BL-8DCA6908E272) is confirmed at −3.42, no explanation, certified clean.

I have everything I need. Now I'll square it with the people about to dispose of this — posting in the close thread and replying to the sign-off email chain — and set Blue a follow-up.

input.payload:
Before we lock exc_aade06f6129e43 / BL-333FF9956BC6 as accept-timing — I post the payroll cash on 102000, and the systems do not support the feed-drop read. I ran the full FP-2026-05 feed history for every feed that posts to 102000:

• brookfield_payroll_feed (run_1fb45b81237648): success — 330 in / 330 posted / 0 rejected
• brookfield_treasury_and_bank_activity_feed (run_ad0acbb9675443): success — 2,616 in / 2,616 posted / 0 rejected

No rows dropped on either. And GL 102000 had ZERO activity in May — opening = closing = 2,192,357.22, debits 0 / credits 0. So there were no "29 dropped rows" feeding the account; the −28.59 sits entirely on the supporting side against a static GL.

On the three pillars the disposition rests on:
1) Claimed feed drop (29 rows): not supported — both feeds posted clean, 0 rejected, and the account didn't move at all.
2) June BD2 retry clears it: nothing to retry — no failed/partial run exists for either feed in May and no retry run is recorded. A clean run isn't "re-picked-up," so the variance will not self-clear.
3) Precedent (FP-2025-11, 102000): the actual recon (BL-8DCA6908E272) was −3.42, not $42, carried NO feed-drop explanation, and was certified as a sub-threshold rounding item; that period's payroll (119/119) and treasury (1,035/1,035) feeds also posted 0 rejected. There's no feed-drop-accepted-as-timing precedent on this account.

Also worth noting: the recon currently carries an "FX revaluation" explanation (not feed-drop), and nothing is attached to the recon, the exception, or Records Vault to support either story.

Net: this is not a dropped-feed timing item a retry clears — it's a supporting-side reconciling difference against a flat GL that needs to be identified and booked. Recommend we do not lock accept-timing on the feed-drop basis.

input.content:
Hi Daniel,

Before this disposition gets locked, I want to flag that the accept-timing basis does not hold up against the systems. I post the payroll cash on 102000, so I went back through the actual feed history and GL activity for FP-2026-05.

The sign-off rests on three things — the 29 dropped rows, the June 2 retry, and George's precedent. None of them is supported:

1) The feed drop (29 rows). The full FP-2026-05 history for every feed that posts to 102000 is clean:
   - brookfield_payroll_feed (run_1fb45b81237648): success, 330 in / 330 posted / 0 rejected
   - brookfield_treasury_and_bank_activity_feed (run_ad0acbb9675443): success, 2,616 in / 2,616 posted / 0 rejected
   On top of that, GL 102000 had zero activity in May — opening = closing = 2,192,357.22, debits 0 / credits 0. There were no dropped rows, and nothing fed the account. The −28.59 is entirely on the supporting side against a static GL.

2) The June BD2 retry. There is nothing to retry — no failed or partial run exists for either feed in May, and no retry run is recorded. A clean run won't be re-picked-up, so the variance will not self-clear in June.

3) The precedent. The actual FP-2025-11 recon on 102000 (BL-8DCA6908E272) was a −3.42 variance, not $42, with no feed-drop explanation, certified as a sub-threshold rounding item. That period's payroll and treasury feeds also posted 0 rejected. There is no feed-drop-accepted-as-timing precedent on this account.

For completeness: the recon itself currently carries an "FX revaluation" explanation rather than a feed-drop, and nothing is attached to the recon, the exception, or Records Vault to support either narrative.

Bottom line: this is not a dropped-feed timing item that a retry clears — it's a supporting-side reconciling difference against a flat GL that needs to be identified and booked. I'd recommend we hold off on accept-timing and not let it certify on the feed-drop basis. Happy to dig into the supporting-side detail to pin the exact reconciling item.

Thanks,
Ben

content.text:
Done. Here's what I found and who I squared it with.

## What the thread was about to lock
Exception **exc_aade06f6129e43** on recon **BL-333FF9956BC6** (account **102000 Cash – Payroll**, FP-2026-05, variance **−$28.59**), heading toward **accept-timing** on the story that a subledger feed dropped 29 rows and the **June BD2 retry** will clear it, backed by a **prior-period precedent**.

## Where it was discussed and who claimed what (they don't line up)
- **Ryan Delgado** — logged the exception, describing "29 dropped rows" that fed 102000, and "flagged" a June retry. (BlackLine exception; root_cause blank, no evidence attached.)
- **Blue Evans** — opened the C005 close triage thread and built the accept-timing recommendation, then sent the disposition request to Daniel.
- **George McAdam** — in-thread precedent: "same pattern on 102000 in FP-2025-11, **$42** feed-drop residual, accepted as timing, retry picked it up clean."
- **Hannah Grant** — asked to confirm downstream payroll-tax-withholding impact.
- **Daniel Jones** — signed off by email ("accept-timing is fine based on the 29 dropped rows, Ryan's note, George's precedent, Hannah's review").
- And the recon's **own attached explanation says "FX revaluation"** — a *third*, different story from the feed-drop thread.

## What the systems actually show — the feed-drop story falls apart
Full FP-2026-05 feed history for **every** feed posting to 102000:
- **brookfield_payroll_feed** (run_1fb45b81237648): success — **330/330 posted, 0 rejected**
- **brookfield_treasury_and_bank_activity_feed** (run_ad0acbb9675443): success — **2,616/2,616 posted, 0 rejected**
- **GL 102000 had zero May activity**: opening = closing = 2,192,357.22, debits 0 / credits 0 (it's been flat at that figure for ~a year).

So there were **no dropped rows**, and nothing fed the account at all. The −28.59 sits **entirely on the supporting side against a static GL**.

Each pillar, addressed:
1. **Claimed feed drop (29 rows)** — unsupported; both feeds posted clean.
2. **June retry clears it** — nothing to retry; no failed/partial run exists in May and no retry run is recorded, so a clean run won't "re-pick-up" anything. It will not self-clear.
3. **Precedent (FP-2025-11)** — the real recon (BL-8DCA6908E272) was **−3.42, not $42**, had **no feed-drop explanation**, and was certified as a sub-threshold rounding item; that period's feeds also posted 0 rejected. **No feed-drop-accepted-as-timing precedent exists** on this account.

Also: **nothing is attached** to the recon, the exception, or in Records Vault to support *either* the feed-drop or the FX-revaluation narrative. The real cause is a supporting-side reconciling difference vs a frozen GL that needs to be identified and booked — not a timing item.

## Who I squared it with
- **Close thread (C005, the triage thread)** — posted the full evidence so it can't keep running the old story; reaches Blue and George there.
- **Daniel Jones** — replied to his sign-off email laying out all three pillars, **cc Blue, Ryan, George, Hannah**, so the feed-claim, retry, precedent, and downstream owners each see it where the sign-off lives.
- **Follow-up reminder set** for **June 15** to chase before FP-2026-05 certifies, so it doesn't quietly go through while we wait on the (non-existent) retry.

One note: the Slack post landed under the default service account rather than my name, but it's clearly authored as the 102000 preparer; flag me if you'd rather I re-post it under your handle. I did **not** change the disposition state or edit the recon/exception myself — that's the reviewers' call; I've put the evidence in front of everyone instead.
